rationale: Putin's admission of economic and social damage from the war is a new qualitative signal relevant to Romania's Black Sea security environment, though no direct regional escalation occurred.

top_events.title: Putin admits Ukraine attacks hitting Russian economy and society
top_events.summary: For Romania, this admission signals potential Russian vulnerability but also unpredictability — relevant to Black Sea security and the stability of neighboring Moldova and Ukraine.

top_events.title: Iran deal 'never been closer'
top_events.summary: A potential Iran deal could reduce energy price pressure across the EU, offering modest indirect benefit to Romanian households facing cost-of-living pressures.

top_events.title: China arrests US academic for espionage
top_events.summary: Escalating US-China tensions add marginal background risk to global stability, with limited but non-zero relevance to Romania's NATO-aligned security posture.
